message: Args and Env entries are only sanitized when the whole string starts with the home directory, leaving home paths embedded in KEY=value env entries or flag/config args unchanged.
evidence: sanitizeHomePath only checks s == home or strings.HasPrefix(s, home+"/"); acpAdapterCommand builds Env as ANTHROPIC_MODEL=<value> and Args as model=<quoted value>, so home paths inside those recorded strings are not replaced with '~'.

message: Recorded args/env values are only sanitized when the whole string starts with the home directory, so composite values like `model="~/me/..."` or `ANTHROPIC_MODEL=~/me/...` can still leak absolute home paths in BuildDryRunPlan/BuildACPWouldRun output.
evidence: sanitizeHomePath checks only `s == home` or `strings.HasPrefix(s, home+"/")`; acpAdapterCommand records codex model pins as `model=%q` args and claude pins as `ANTHROPIC_MODEL=` env strings, where the home path would appear after a prefix.

message: BuildDryRunPlan and recorded Args/Env sanitization are not covered by the added tests. The new test exercises BuildACPWouldRun.Command and the scalar helper, but no test drives the BuildDryRunPlan WouldRun path or asserts sanitization of path-containing Args/Env entries in recorded dry-run output.
evidence: internal/agents/dryrun.go sanitizes BuildDryRunPlan at lines 85-88, while internal/agents/dryrun_test.go only defines TestBuildACPWouldRun and TestSanitizeHomePath; existing app dry-run tests assert default npx command shapes, not home-path sanitization through BuildDryRunPlan or Args/Env.

message: The docs still describe execute plan as writing the exact command, but dry-run recording now sanitizes home-directory prefixes to '~' in Command, Args, and Env. Update the user-facing docs to clarify that the recorded would_run command may be home-path redacted.
evidence: docs/flow.md:170 says the plan writes the exact command; internal/agents/dryrun.go:55-57 and 86-88 now pass recorded fields through sanitizeHomePath/sanitizeHomePaths, whose comment says only the recorded representation is sanitized.
